commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed because the blocker is a security-policy boundary around whether hostname allowlists should imply private-network trust.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete SSRF hardening regression by treating hostname allowlists as resolved private-IP bypasses.; Review finding: [P1] Keep hostname allowlists from bypassing private DNS checks — src/infra/net/ssrf.ts:159-161 (sha=8c5cec2a7cc3f394ed94555d1611cce898ddee12)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes SSRF hostname and private-network checks so policy hostname allowlists can permit `.internal`, `.localhost`, and `.local` hostnames, with regression tests for those hostname cases.

Reproducibility: yes. for the no-private-opt-in path by source inspection: current main blocks `.internal` hostnames when only the auto-generated `hostnameAllowlist` is present and checks private DNS answers. It is unclear for the linked issue's `allowPrivateNetwork` claim because the shown config omits `request.allowPrivateNetwork`, and current main appears to pass that option through when present.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the blocker is a security-policy boundary around whether hostname allowlists should imply private-network trust.

target.title: fix(ssrf): allow hostnameAllowlist to bypass .internal/.localhost/.local blocks
target.author: dchekmarev

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
